candidates.why: This D&A row belongs to Sales and Marketing, not to the selected COGS bucket 'Cost of revenues/COGS'.
This D&A row belongs to R&D, not to the selected COGS bucket 'Cost of revenues/COGS'.
This appears within the Administration section rather than as a COGS-related D&A line.
This is administration-related D&A, not COGS-related.
A generic depreciation row is shown without values and without clear linkage to COGS; conservative treatment is not to add it to COGS.

variables.notes: No explicit operating D&A row in the excerpt is tied to cost of revenue / COGS. Therefore COGS D&A is assumed embedded in the selected 'Cost of revenues/COGS' row, and no separate add-back row should be selected.

candidates.why: This is explicitly a D&A sub-component of the already selected parent row 'Sales and Marketing', so adding it separately would double count.

This is explicitly a D&A sub-component of the already selected parent row 'Research and development'. Because 'Research and development' is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, adding this separately would be unnecessary and risks distortion under the stated R&D rule.
This appears within the Administration block and is best interpreted as a component of the already selected parent row 'Administration', not a separate same-level SG&A D&A total to add.
This is explicitly a D&A sub-component of the already selected parent row 'Administration', so it should not be added separately.
The generic 'Depreciation' row has no values and no clear bucket assignment; it should not be added separately given more specific D&A component rows already exist within selected SG&A buckets.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket is the combined sum of 'Sales and Marketing', 'Research and development', and 'Administration'. The visible D&A rows are presented as components inside those already selected parent rows rather than as separate same-level operating expense lines. 'PPA amortization' also appears within Administration. To avoid double counting, no separate XSGA D&A row should be added.
